source_intake_record.raw_question_text: e. The electromagnet is composed of layers of spiral wound copper pipe; the pipe has inner diameter $d_{i}=0.40 \mathrm{~cm}$ and outer diameter $d_{o}=0.50 \mathrm{~cm}$. The copper pipe is wound into this flat spiral that has an inner diameter $D_{i}=20 \mathrm{~cm}$ and outer diameter $D_{o}=50 \mathrm{~cm}$. Assuming the pipe almost touches in the spiral winding, determine the length $L$ in one spiral.

multi_step: To solve, one must combine multiple given dimensions: spiral inner/outer diameters and pipe outer diameter.
The task likely requires determining the number of windings from radial span and then aggregating circumferences or using an equivalent spiral-length approximation.
This is more than direct lookup or one-step substitution; it involves at least two linked reasoning/calculation steps.
verifiability: Multiple-choice options are provided, enabling straightforward final-answer checking.
registry_passed=true with no listed asset issues
The computation is formula-based and can be independently re-derived from the stated quantities.
